items.summary: Fix backend admin authentication so that calling `adminLogin(password)` accepts the exact admin password `JATINSHARMA2580` (ignoring accidental leading/trailing whitespace in the submitted password), returns a non-empty admin session token on success, and returns an explicit invalid-password result when the password is incorrect.

Ensure the backend exposes the admin session APIs expected by the frontend contract and that they behave consistently: `adminLogin(password)` for issuing tokens and `validateAdminSession(token)` for validating them. A token returned from `adminLogin` must be accepted by `validateAdminSession` until it expires, and validation must refresh the token’s last-seen timestamp according to the backend’s session timeout rules.

Make the admin password value persist correctly across canister upgrades so upgrades do not revert/retain an unintended previous admin password. If upgrade handling is required under the project migration policy, add/adjust the backend upgrade/migration logic to preserve existing state while ensuring the stored admin password is set to `JATINSHARMA2580` after upgrade.

Verify the admin login flow works end-to-end in the UI using the existing `AdminLoginCard` + `useAdminSession` behavior: entering the correct password successfully authenticates and shows the admin dashboard; entering an incorrect password shows exactly "Invalid password. Please try again."; backend/method/candid failures show a service-unavailable style message in English.
